rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing output features.
Declare auxiliary variables with explicit types to match expression results.
Avoid referencing AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex agree blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Component type contracts govern port behaviors; implementation contracts handle subcomponent bindings.
Boolean expressions in AGREE must align with actual data types of referenced features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use eq for auxiliary variable declarations with explicit type annotations.
Wrap AGREE annex content in annex agree {** ... **}; blocks.
Ensure all identifiers in assumptions and guarantees are declared or visible in scope.
Place implementation-specific assigns in component implementation annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not place output guarantees in implementation scope.
Do not reference AADL timing properties directly as AGREE variables.
Do not assume Boolean ports can be treated as integer values.
Do not generate clauses without verifying visible feature availability.
